middleware.description: Guards the public webhook plane (POST /webhooks/payment/{provider}). Extracts the provider's signature header + verifies HMAC-SHA256 over the request body + timestamp per per-provider signing scheme (Stripe: `Stripe-Signature`; Paddle: `Paddle-Signature`; Adyen: `HmacSignature`; Square: `Square-Signature`; PayPal: `Paypal-Transmission-Sig` with cert verification; CustomWebhook: `X-Payment-Signature`). Refuses (400) with `payment_webhook_signature_invalid` on failure. No body echoed on refuse. Runs BEFORE any tenant/application resolution (the webhook body carries the tenant identifier).

consumed.note: Applied on tenant + platform-admin plane routes. NOT applied on the webhook plane (webhook body carries the provider's tenant identifier).
Applied on tenant plane. Webhook plane resolves tenant from the verified webhook body.

Applied per-route with the specific entitlement key (payment_capture for base surface, payment_manual_capture on POST /payments/{payment}/capture, payment_dispute_management on /payment-disputes/*, etc.).
The permission-based authorize middleware wraps the ability checks defined in policies.json + permissions.json. Route-level `permission` metadata resolves through this middleware.
Consent gate is NOT applied on payment routes — payment processing is a contractual data-processing activity under GDPR Art. 6(1)(b), not consent-based. Customers cannot opt out of a bill they owe.
